Katrina is furious that the well in her garden has dried up, and gamekeeper Golly loses his knack of wooing the opposite sex. Richard Briers and Susan Hampshire star

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: CID officers attend the funeral of murdered supermodel Cindy Statham, only to learn that killer Larry Franks has been transferred to hospital following a suicide bid. When he makes a break for freedom, a frantic search of the building ensues and Beth is first to pick up the trail of blood. Smithy's first day as Acting Inspector goes from bad to worse when he calls in the Firearms Command to sort out a building society robbery by two bungling ex-employees

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Joseph goes too far when voicing his suspicions about Lauren, leaving Vanessa with no option but to threaten him with suspension. Michael fears the removal of the robotics equipment when he learns an inspector is due to visit and no one has been hired to stand in for Connie, while Penny's compassion for Scott after his readmission to AAU lands her in trouble with Judith

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Harry tries to raise morale on the ward following Jim's death and Abs manages to break down Nina's defences when they are forced to treat a patient together. Ellen receives unwelcome news about her visa, Bex struggles to deal with Selena's constant bullying and Claire comes up with a solution to Comfort's legal woes. With Simon MacCorkindale, James Redmond and Georgina Bouzova

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Larry Franks withdraws his confession to the murder of supermodel Cindy Statham and Jo reopens the case, convinced they have the wrong man in custody. Crime scene photographer Rachel Inns helps her work late into the night re-examining the evidence. Mickey investigates a smash-and-grab raid on a jewellery shop and ends up arresting an old friend, Mod cafe owner Jerry Harper, whose daughter owns the scooter used as a getaway vehicle

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Ricky wants to get back together with Bianca, but she'll have to think about it. Pauline organises a funeral and Alan proposes to Carol

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: There's bad news for Barry. Ricky is in for a surprise. Pauline admits the truth about her visits to Arthur. Gita pays Guppy a last visit and Alan and Carol argue over his proposal

programme.title: Soldier, Soldier
programme.desc: The Fusiliers and their families arrive in Hong Kong, but have a hard time adjusting to their new surroundings and the climate. Starring Jerome Flynn and Robson Green

programme.title: Pie in the Sky
programme.desc: Henry Crabbe takes on a group of conservationists who are opposed to the building of a new motorway link. Culinary drama, starring Richard Griffiths

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: James and Siegfried react very differently when the Veterinary Record publishes one of Calum's articles. Rural drama based upon the novels of James Herriot, starring Christopher Timothy, Robert Hardy and John McGlynn

programme.title: To the Manor Born
programme.desc: Audrey finds herself under suspicion after a valuable piece of DeVere's antique china mysteriously disappears while she helps him catalogue his collection. Comedy, starring Penelope Keith and Peter Bowles

programme.title: Are You Being Served?
programme.desc: Shoplifting at Grace Brothers is on the increase so Mr Rumbold installs a CCTV camera to tackle the problem, but the staff suspect it is not intended for the customers. Shop-floor comedy, starring Mollie Sugden, John Inman and Nicholas Smith

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Hobbo, Alvin and Entwistle set out to save the day when Howard suspects Pearl is seeing someone else and the trio soon draw up a surveillance operation that leads them to a seedy bikers' club. Toby sets out to find Aunty Wainwright when he has ironing problems

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: After suffering a brush with Stella, Hobbo comes to the conclusion that it is time to find her a man - and, with the help of Clegg and Truly, he decides that eligible village heart-throb Norris Fairburn is the right match. Pearl's new-found passion for motorcycling causes trouble for Howard and Marina

programme.title: Hetty Wainthropp Investigates
programme.desc: An arsonist terrorises a housing estate, and Hetty goes undercover in a quilt-making circle in her search for the culprit, while Robert is confined at home with a broken collarbone and has to content himself with Polaroid shots of his burgeoning runner bean crop

programme.title: Life on Mars
programme.desc: Fantasy drama, starring John Simm as a detective who ends up in a coma while pursuing a serial killer - and when he comes round, he finds he has somehow been transported back to 1973. As he attempts to uncover what has happened to him, he must also contend with the macho behaviour of his Sweeneyesque colleagues, as well as a murderer whose crimes are oddly similar to the one he was investigating in his own time

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: A reluctant Brian enters an addiction centre run by monks to treat his alcoholism, but soon becomes bored and looks for a distraction. He finds one when a chance remark leads the team to reopen an investigation into the death of a heroin user nine years previously - and to help reveal the truth, Gerry is sent undercover as a sex addict. Richard Wilson and Fay Ripley guest star

programme.title: Waking the Dead
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. The team is called in to investigate when a convicted killer is cleared of the murder of his adoptive father on appeal. Not fully convinced the man is innocent, Boyd tries to find the rest of the family to establish the truth, but when he does eventually track the relatives down, he realises they have a lot to hide. Drama, starring Trevor Eve, Sue Johnston and Claire Goose

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Margherita Taylor helps a woman in North Yorkshire who has £500,000 to find a country property back in her home county. During her time there, Margherita hits the production line at an organic farm which specialises in seasonal fruit-and-veg boxes

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Two sets of farmers - David and George, and Liam and Alice - hope to make a splash with their premium ciders and harvested vegetables at a market in Ludlow

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Nigel Havers hosts as five dealers bid against each other for items brought in by the public, including an unusual Asian drum, an Argentinian belt and charming push-a-long toy

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Magazine show, with stories of interest from around the UK, plus celebrity guests in the studio, presented by Alex Jones

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Nigel Havers presents a programme in which five dealers bid against one another to buy intriguing items brought in by members of the public. The second episode features wannabe dealer Alfie, aged just 11, as he tries to sell his scooter to the dealers, and an unusual birthing chair and a pair of vintage phones come the dealers' way

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Bike restorer Tim Gunn brings all his skill to bear on a wartime bicycle once owned and used by a Bletchley Park codebreaker. Horologist Steve Fletcher recruits fellow expert and sister Suzie to work on a neglected and battered Victorian firefighter's helmet. Woodworker Will Kirk and silversmith Brenton West team up to repair a 19th-century stereoscope viewer that sparked a lifelong obsession, and soft toy rest toy restorers Amanda Middleditch and Julie Tatchell try to make a faceless old teddy bear smile again

programme.title: The Truth About
programme.desc: With millions of people swapping the gym for the living room during the Covid-19 pandemic, Mehreen Baig delves into the latest science to find the best way to get fit at home. She uncovers exciting new research testing the theory that just one minute of strength training per week on each main muscle group might reduce your risk of diabetes and heart disease, and also looks at the impact wearable fitness tech actually has on our willpower to stick to an exercise plan. Later, she investigates whether popular supplements like pre-trainers and protein shakes really work

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Highlights of the latest Premier League matches, which included Aston Villa v Tottenham Hotspur and Wolverhampton Wanderers v Everton. Plus, highlights of Sheffield United v Newcastle United and Manchester City v Brighton & Hove Albion

programme.title: Fight for First: Excel Esports
programme.desc: Documentary following Excel Esports owner Kieran Holmes-Darby and his team as they fight to be taken seriously in the global video games scene

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Former Premier League midfielder Steve Sidwell, Winter Olympic snowboarder Amy Fuller, world indoor champion sprinter Richard Kitty and netball star Pamela Cookey join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather. Presented by Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty

programme.title: Rip Off Britain: Holidays
programme.desc: Angela Rippon, Gloria Hunniford and Julia Somerville focus on the devastating impact of coronavirus on travel, including the insurance company that refused to pay out after a couple's grand tour of South America came to a grinding halt. They also speak to a customer who was forced to turn to local police for help after her online travel agency failed to answer its 24-hour emergency helpline

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The presenters visit two London properties - a two-bedroom flat in Bexley and a large Victorian house in Wimbledon - as well as a semi-detached house in Kidsgrove, Staffordshire

programme.title: Wanted Down Under Revisited
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman catches up with Gareth and Jodi Todd, three years after they spent a trial week in Australia, to find out where they and their family now call home

programme.title: The Moment of Proof
programme.desc: How a satnav revealed the mastermind behind the biggest armed robbery ever to hit Devon and Cornwall, and a hoodie helped police take a dangerous attacker off the streets

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: The red and blue teams vie at Brighton Racecourse, East Sussex, hunting for items of value with the help of experts Catherine Southon and Mark Stacey. Presenter Christina Trevanion maintains order and also gets to meet a gramophone guru, learning how to spot the difference between an authentic gramophone and a replica

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: The Kembleford Ramblers' annual outing takes a tragic turn when the local lord is found strangled, so the cleric gets his walking boots on to solve the crime. Murder mystery, starring Mark Williams

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Comfort Food
programme.desc: Everyday ingredients transformed, including bananas used as the basis of a sophisticated tarte-tatin and light Italian dumplings with simple ricotta cheese

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin helps a couple search for a property in Dorset on a budget of £650,000, and later visits an award-winning dairy farm putting the county's cheeses on the culinary map

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Two couples are preparing for Exeter's Farmers' Market. Ex-archaeologist Janet and her husband Andy are hoping to sell some of their chickens to make enough cash to finish Uncle Pat's new home on their farm. Meanwhile, Liv and Henry are selling their vegetables. They have big plans to create an outdoor kitchen on their modest seven-acre farm, and with some exciting news on the horizon, this young couple are praying for all the sales they can get. Narrated by Denis Lawson

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones presents a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Jean forges ahead with her plan and offers words of wisdom to a struggling Sheree. Shirley is determined to put her family first and wants answers. Martin finally realises what he wants

programme.title: Pooch Perfect
programme.desc: Sheridan Smith and her canine co-host Stanley open the doors of the Pooch Parlour to four more professional dog groomers out to prove their skills. First they tackle a pack of Alaskan malamutes, whose fur well and truly flies when the contestants get to work de-shedding their undercoats. They then give four fluffy puppies their first ever haircut to be revealed in front of their proud owners. Verity Hardcastle and Colin Taylor judge their efforts and decide who is going through to the next round

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: An archaeologist is poisoned during a dig, and with only two suspects and a quick confession, it looks like this is going to be an open-and-shut case. However, it turns out the archaeologists were not alone - and the team realise they have little to no evidence. Meanwhile, Neville and Florence realise they might get along better than they originally thought, although the DI's attempts to embrace island life do not find favour with the Commissioner

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate, inviting a panel of politicians and other guests to answer questions from a virtual audience on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week

programme.title: Supermarket Secrets
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace goes behind the scenes with Britain's biggest food retailers to discover how technology and science are being used to bring consumers their food. In the first episode, Gregg examines the ways that supermarkets are fighting to be first with the latest food fashions, and discovers how online shopping is transforming the way people purchase their food and what they put in their trolleys. He joins Tesco's Head of Plant Innovation as he invents new vegan meals that he claims will taste like meat, and also finds out how Asda are using ingenious growing techniques to make their tomatoes sweeter than strawberries

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather. Presented by Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty

programme.title: Rip Off Britain: Holidays
programme.desc: The travellers still fighting for refunds months after their holidays were cancelled, and a couple asked to pay out for a transatlantic flight to a cruise that never took place

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The presenters follow the development progress of a corner plot in Wallasey, Merseyside, a mid-terrace house in Hyde in Manchester and a bungalow in Great Barr, Birmingham

programme.title: Wanted Down Under Revisited
programme.desc: Andrew Treagust needed a change of pace and thought this lay in New Zealand, where he could follow his dream of becoming a snowboard instructor. Partner Emma was less than convinced, having a job she loved in the UK and two young boys with close ties to their grandparents. Six years after the family sampled a week 'down under', the programme catches up with them to see how their lives have changed. Presented by Nicki Chapman

programme.title: The Moment of Proof
programme.desc: How a distinctive deer stalker hat was the red flag to cracking a web of stolen bank cards. Plus, how one careless text message put a dangerous stalker behind bars for the longest sentence in a police force's history. Narrated by Chuku Modu

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Host Eric Knowles enters the world of travel and transport, as he takes a look back at some of the most interesting past purchases in that genre. He also meets a group of students and their treasured vintage fire engine, dubbed Jezebel, and offers viewers advice on how to spot a bargain

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: When Lady Felicia's home comes under threat, Father Brown is forced to seek help from an unlikely ally. Mark Williams and Nancy Carroll star

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Comfort Food
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers cook dishes for when the temperature drops and the weather calls for sugar and spice and good hearty cooking. This means oxtail soup with herby dumplings and a new take on an old favourite, treacle sponge

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Margherita Taylor heads to Rutland, England's smallest county, to help a young mother to carve out a new country life for her family with a budget of £450,000. Margherita also visits Rutland Water, where she gets a hands-on course in fly-fishing to find out what she might catch for dinner

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: After an early frost decimated a big part of their crop, father-and-son team Billy and Billy Junior are excited to sell a fair chunk of their organic fruit and nuts at Birmingham's Moseley Farmers' Market. Meanwhile, farmer Leon is hoping his brother-in-law Peter will pull in the punters to try and then buy some of their home-grown, home-made wines and gins. Narrated by Denis Lawson

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Nigel Havers and expert auctioneer Simon Bower welcome five more people to the Bidding Room with another batch of weird and wonderful lots. The dealers bid against each other on a fairground dodgem, two figurines, a pair of ornate candlesticks, a vintage garden sprayer and a rescued mannequin

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz show in which pairs of contestants have to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions posed to 100 people before the show. At the end of each round, the team with the most points is eliminated until the final two pairs battle for a cash prize. Presented by Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and a co-host present the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Dave and Harry Rich head to West Sussex, where they come up with ideas for the owners of a narrow garden who want to modernise their space

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Captain Matt Dawson is joined by British athletics stars Adam Gemili and Eilidh Doyle, while Phil Tufnell teams up with former footballers Leon Osman and Stephen Warnock. Sue Barker presents

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Denise fears the worst after hearing the truth. Gray struggles with Karen's new role, while a distraught Kathy is at a loss over what to do next

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Snooker player Stephen Hendry, presenter Laura Whitmore and comedian-actors Chris McCausland and Maisie Adam join host Rob Brydon and regular captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show. As usual, the participants set out to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible fibs about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory, through rounds including Home Truths and Quick-fire Lies

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: To celebrate their anniversary, Lee and Lucy plan a friendly night of quizzing with Toby and Anna in their local pub. However, the evening is derailed when neither couple can bear to lose to their partners and the foursome splits into rival teams. Comedy, starring Lee Mack and Sally Bretton, with Hugh Dennis

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: Bodyguard and Durrells star Keeley Hawes joins the host to discuss her new drama series Finding Alice, while writer and one-time Doctor Who co-star Noel Clarke talks about his cop drama Bulletproof: South Africa. Hollywood star Rebel Wilson chats about Straight Talking, which sees her collaborate with former soldier Ant Middleton, and eminent film-maker M Night Shyamalan promotes his new psychological horror series Servant. Comedian Rob Beckett also guests and Jake Bugg performs his current single All I Need

programme.title: Young Adult (2011)
programme.desc: A selfish teenager grows up to be an alcoholic failed writer. When she learns her high-school sweetheart's wife has just had a baby, she refuses to believe he can be happy without her, and returns to her home town to steal him away from his happy family, finding an unlikely ally in a classmate whose adolescence she made a misery. Comedy, starring Charlize Theron, Patrick Wilson and Patton Oswalt

programme.title: RuPaul's Drag Race UK
programme.desc: Ahead of the much-anticipated second series, Mama Ru introduces the 12 contestants competing for the title of the UK's next Drag Race superstar

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Wanted Down Under Revisited
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman catches up with Stef Barker, who had been trying to convince her husband to move to New Zealand with their family

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Two farming families gear up for the horse carriage marathon at the Ashfield's Carriage and Polo Club in Essex, including a past national competitor in the junior championships

programme.title: See Hear
programme.desc: Nadeem Islam meets Jean Pentland, who takes him on a tour of the Scottish Highlands, where he samples local cuisine, history and culture. With voiceover

programme.title: Nigella's Cook, Eat, Repeat
programme.desc: Nigella Lawson shares recipes that can be made in advance for cheesecake ice-cream and a fish stew, as well as a fried chicken sandwich garnished with some of her favourite condiments. Nigella also makes a simple yet daring dish of cherry flambé using frozen fruit and kirsch, and uses up the final slices of a soft white loaf to prepare rum-spiked French toast

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Update on politics from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: BBC Bitesize Daily
programme.desc: Chemistry is the focus of the day, plus a look at the life of Marie Curie

programme.title: The Planets
programme.desc: Professor Brian Cox examines Jupiter, the largest and oldest planet in the solar system, revealing how its immense gravity influenced where other planets developed, destroying several nascent planets and negatively influencing the development of Mars. Today, Jupiter's great mass continues to be felt. Its gravity torments its moon Io, creating the most volcanically active body in the solar system. And through the vice-like grip it exerts over the asteroid belt, Jupiter's influence extends as far as Earth

programme.title: Live Snooker: The Masters
programme.desc: Ronnie O'Sullivan v Ding Junhui. Day four at Marshall Arena in Milton Keynes gets under way with the seventh first-round match, with a quarter-final against John Higgins or Mark Allen awaiting the winner. This is the fifth time the pair have met in the Masters and the Rocket has been victorious on each occasion, and has lifted the trophy a record seven times

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Caroline Hawley and Adam Partridge value items brought to Muncaster Castle, near Ravenglass in Cumbria, while Paul Martin finds out about the Lake District's bobbin industry

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: David Baddiel, Alex Brooker, Sophie Duker and Charlotte Hawkins go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: Rick Stein's Cornwall
programme.desc: On the wild north Atlantic coast, Rick heads out to sea to go fishing for lobsters with his old friend and celebrity chef Nathan Outlaw, before helping to cook up a lobster risotto. Later, at Tresillian House near Newquay, Rick meets with head gardener John Harris to find out more about his Victorian walled garden

programme.title: Cornwall: This Fishing Life
programme.desc: Britain's departure from the EU brings changes for Newlyn, Cornwall's biggest fishing port. The harbourmaster oversees a redevelopment of the fish market, as well as making plans to transform the harbour's ageing infrastructure. However, many fishermen resent his grand plans, feeling that they were not consulted over massive changes to their working environment. While many fishermen supported leaving the EU to gain control over quotas, many now fear that their industry will be sacrificed during negotiations

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: Hill farmer Joyce Campbell prepares spring, and does everything she can to make sure her 800 North Country Cheviot ewes are healthy ahead of birthing season. Meanwhile in the Lake District, Matt and Dani Blair are preparing for the first cattle TB test on their new farm, and in Aberdeenshire, spring has come early for new entrant farmers Isla and George at Little Rowater Farm

programme.title: Death in Bollywood
programme.desc: After two new enquiries conclude that Jiah Khan had died by suicide, the actress's family commission a legal case review from a British law firm, which finds there were faults with the police investigations from the night she died onwards and that vital evidence may have been missed

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1987
programme.desc: Some of 1987's biggest music stars look back on the year, which saw Margaret Thatcher secure a third term as prime minister, while the Channel Tunnel began construction and Britain was battered by the Great Storm. While those events unfolded in the outside world, the TOTP studio provided a haven for those who wanted to escape. Stock, Aitken and Waterman provided the floor-fillers as hip-hop and rap made their mark on the chart alongside the homegrown House genre. Contributors include Terence Trent D'Arby, Curiosity Killed the Cat, Rick Astley, Kim Appleby, Andy Bell, Carol Decker and Belinda Carlisle

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, with Emily Maitlis

programme.title: Snooker: The Masters
programme.desc: John Higgins v Mark Allen. Action from the concluding first-round match at Marshall Arena in Milton Keynes, between the Wizard of Wishaw and the Pistol

programme.title: Snooker: The Masters - Extra
programme.desc: Ronnie O'Sullivan v Ding Junhui and John Higgins v Mark Allen. A look back at the key moments from day four at Marshall Arena in Milton Keynes

programme.title: See Hear
programme.desc: Nadeem Islam meets Jean Pentland, who takes him on a tour of the Scottish Highlands, where he samples local cuisine, history and culture. With voiceover

programme.title: Dynasties
programme.desc: A young meerkat queen lives in the harsh beauty of the Madgadikgadi salt pans. If her new pups are to survive, she must unite her family in the face of rivals, predators and immense dust storms. Narrated by David Attenborough

programme.title: The Moment of Proof
programme.desc: How a discarded scrap of paper found in a household bin helped convict a killer, and a distinctive mole holds the key to tracking down a dangerous and predatory attacker

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Wanted Down Under Revisited
programme.desc: Catching up with the Graham family three years after they appeared on the show to see if they decided to leave the UK behind for a new life in Australia

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Two sets of farmers - David and George, and Liam and Alice - hope to make a splash with their premium ciders and harvested vegetables at a market in Ludlow

programme.title: 12 Puppies and Us
programme.desc: Linus the Bedlington terrier joins the Seymour family, but mum Rebecca is not sure she has made the right decision. Cookie the French bulldog moves in with hairdresser Louise, personal trainer Phil and their two young boys. The family have high hopes she will fit in with their active lifestyle, but are thrown when she refuses to go out on walks. In n West Yorkshire, Phoebe the cavapoo has now been with retirees Linda and Andrew for a month and shows no signs of slowing down

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Update on politics from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: BBC Bitesize Daily
programme.desc: Bitesize blasts into space and discovers what it is like to live in orbit

programme.title: The Planets
programme.desc: Professor Brian Cox examines Saturn, a planet raised in the freezing outer reaches of the solar system that began life as a strange combination of rock and ice. Born outside the snow line, with an abundance of building materials, it soon grew to dwarf Earth, drawing in colossal amounts of the hydrogen and helium that permeated the early solar system. As Nasa's Cassini probe has discovered, the planet remained ring-less most of its life, until a fateful encounter changed everything. Less than a hundred million years ago, one of Saturn's ice moons was drawn too close to the planet. In a truly cataclysmic event, the entire moon was destroyed and the rings were born

programme.title: Live Snooker: The Masters
programme.desc: David Gilbert v Kyren Wilson. Coverage of the opening quarter-final at Marshall Arena in Milton Keynes

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: The team heads to Norwich Cathedral, where Thomas Plant and Kate Bateman unearth items including a religious relic and a Martin Brothers vase, and Paul Martin visits the circus

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: David Baddiel, Alex Brooker, Sophie Duker and Charlotte Hawkins test their skills in a variety of trivia-based games, with the ultimate winner declared on Friday

programme.title: Rick Stein's Cornwall
programme.desc: At Golitha Falls, where the River Fowey tumbles down to the sea, Rick meets Dawn French, who has made Cornwall her home. In the shadow of Devonport Dockyard, he boards the world's busiest chain ferry to cross the wide River Tamar, the ancient watery boundary between Devon and Cornwall - along the way discovering an unusual event involving circus elephant. And he meets a man who thinks he has discovered the next superfood, which Rick used to cook up a delicious duck dish

programme.title: Live Snooker: The Masters
programme.desc: Stuart Bingham v Shaun Murphy. Further coverage of the fifth day's play at Marshall Arena in Milton Keynes, featuring the second quarter-final. Bingham is the reigning champion, having lifted the trophy for the first time in his career at Alexandra Palace last year

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: A teenage paddleboarder is being swept out to sea off the coast of north Wales, so the crew of the Abersoch lifeboat race to reach him before he is lost for ever. The crew of the Lizard lifeboat station face two missions, as they are called out to a cargo ship taking on water and in danger of sinking with its crew still on board, followed by a small yacht being battered by a storm in the Atlantic

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Bill Bailey, Jack Carroll and Olga Koch join regular Alan Davies as Sandi Toksvig hosts the quiz with a difference, asking questions on the theme of rogue

programme.title: Mock the Week
programme.desc: Dara O Briain hosts as Ed Gamble, Ed Byrne, Maisie Adam, Rhys James and Ria Lina join regular panellist Hugh Dennis to take a comic look back on the latest events in news and politics. Once again, stand-up and improvisation are the name of the game as the contestants try to get one up on one another

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Mock the Week chairman Dara O Briain comperes the comedy show at London's Hammersmith Apollo. He warms up the audience before introducing routines by stand-up stars Felicity Ward and Mo Gilligan

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Kirsty Wark

programme.title: Snooker: The Masters - Extra
programme.desc: Action from the opening two quarter-finals at Marshall Arena in Milton Keynes, where the first players to win six frames would progress to Saturday's semi-finals

programme.title: The Noughties
programme.desc: Angela Scanlon is joined by Gemma Collins and Joe Swash to take a look at the year's highlights

programme.title: Celebrity: A 21st Century Story
programme.desc: Charting the story of celebrity in the late noughties, the second episode looks at how different groups cashed in on the public's insatiable appetite for access to the famous. Young celebrities were commoditised, criticised and discarded by the people who profited from them, until a new breed of celebrity emerged, ready to wrestle control back for themselves. As the demand for the public to have access to their idols' every move increased, celebrity magazine circulation exploded, and celebrities found themselves up for scrutiny by a new type of showbiz journalism

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Wanted Down Under Revisited
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman catches up with Gareth and Jodi Todd, three years after they spent a trial week in Australia, to find out where they and their family now call home

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Two couples are preparing for Exeter's Farmers' Market. Ex-archaeologist Janet and her husband Andy are hoping to sell some of their chickens to make enough cash to finish Uncle Pat's new home on their farm. Meanwhile, Liv and Henry are selling their vegetables. They have big plans to create an outdoor kitchen on their modest seven-acre farm, and with some exciting news on the horizon, this young couple are praying for all the sales they can get. Narrated by Denis Lawson

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team tackle a concertina that survived combat in the First World War, a vintage jigsaw, and a cocktail table with a surprising secret compartment. Plus, soft-toy restoration duo Julie Tatchell and Amanda Middleditch are called upon to rescue a much-loved toy dog, made by German manufacturer Steiff, which its owner Anita has treasured ever since it was given to her by her grandparents as a young girl

programme.title: BBC Bitesize Daily
programme.desc: Science week comes to a close with physics, focusing on forces, friction and inertia. The Big Read concludes and the Awesome Scientist of the Day is Isaac Newton

programme.title: The Planets
programme.desc: Professor Brian Cox examines the remotest part of the solar system, a place that the most mysterious planets - Uranus, Neptune and dwarf planet Pluto - call home. Barely visible to the naked eye, Uranus was once thought to be the furthest planet from the Sun, before Neptune was discovered. Thanks to a rare alignment of the planets in 1976, Voyager 2 was sent for mankind's only flyby of these ice worlds. Even at such cold temperatures, great storms whip around these frozen worlds, which are home to spectacular moons and intricate ring systems. Then in 2015, Nasa's New Horizons probe pushed the frontier even further into space with its extraordinary passage to the dwarf planet Pluto

programme.title: Live Snooker: The Masters
programme.desc: John Higgins or Mark Allen v Ronnie O'Sullivan or Ding Junhui. Coverage of the third quarter-final at Marshall Arena in Milton Keynes, played over the best of 11 frames

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin presents from the Grand Pier at Weston-super-Mare in Somerset, where experts Catherine Southon and Jonathan Pratt pick out some gems to value. Among them are rare medals that once belonged to an early aviator and puppets that look like the Swedish band Abba. Plus, Paul delves into the history of seaside entertainment.

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: David Baddiel, Alex Brooker, Sophie Duker and Charlotte Hawkins go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games. Their scores are tallied across the week resulting in an overall champion

programme.title: Rick Stein's Cornwall
programme.desc: Rick heads into the county's china clay pits to discover what is known locally as white gold, one of its most important industries. He then climbs the Cornish Alps, huge slag heaps created by the clay mining and some of Cornwall's highest land. After a wild swim at one of Cornwall's picturesque hidden coves, Rick cooks a simple sea bass recipe, revealing how to make the perfect mayonnaise. In the far west of the county, on the Land's End peninsula, Rick meets his niece, a modern artist, who explains her deep spiritual connection to Cornwall

programme.title: Live Snooker: The Masters
programme.desc: Mark Selby or Stephen Maguire v Neil Robertson or Yan Bingtao. The players take to the table at Marshall Arena in Milton Keynes for the fourth and concluding quarter-final. Selby and Robertson were seeded to meet at this stage, and both have been in excellent form in recent weeks

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Part two of two. Monty Don and the team look back at the horticultural highlights from 2020 and celebrate the very best inspirational gardens and gardeners up and down the country. There is also another chance to see what viewers were getting up to in their gardens throughout the year

programme.title: Dolly Parton - Here I Am
programme.desc: An intimate portrait of the acclaimed songwriter, which invites viewers to get to know the real Dolly Parton, the woman behind the global phenomenon. The feature-length documentary, directed by Francis Whately, takes the audience on a musical journey through some of her greatest songs, including The Bridge, I Will Always Love You, and 9 To 5. Cameras also capture Dolly celebrating the 50th anniversary of her very first performance at the Grand Ole Opry, and features unprecedented access to the singer herself, as well as collaborators from throughout her career

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Faisal Islam, the BBC's Economics Editor, reviews the day's important national and international news stories

programme.title: Battlestar Galactica
programme.desc: Part two of two. Starbuck returns from the Twelve Colonies and someone aboard Galactica is revealed to be a Cylon. Roslin suspects Baltar of collaboration with the enemy, but fails to keep the presidency out of his hands. Believing the fleet finally free of the Cylon menace, he decrees the immediate colonisation of New Caprica

programme.title: Snooker: The Masters - Extra
programme.desc: Action from the concluding quarter-finals at Marshall Arena in Milton Keynes, where the players needed to win six frames to progress

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: In-depth current affairs report covering a story behind the headlines

programme.title: The Truth About
programme.desc: A guide to strengthening the immune system during cold and flu season, examining some of the so-called 'miracle' products, superfoods and supplements that claim to help

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Live Snooker: The Masters
programme.desc: John Higgins v Mark Allen. Coverage of the concluding first-round match at Marshall Arena in Milton Keynes, between the Wizard of Wishaw and the Pistol

programme.title: Mrs Brown (1997)
programme.desc: Queen Victoria is helped by a no-nonsense Scottish manservant to overcome her long period of mourning for the death of her husband. However, it is not long before their subsequent friendship is frowned upon by members of the court. John Madden's period drama, starring Judi Dench, Billy Connolly, Geoffrey Palmer, Antony Sher and Gerard Butler

programme.title: Timewatch
programme.desc: Kate Williams explores the story of the monarch who brought Great Britain into the modern age. She examines Victoria's battle to become Queen, in which she fought against her mother, the Duchess of Kent, who wanted to ascend the throne herself - all at a time when the country was on the brink of revolution

programme.title: Queen Victoria: My Musical Britain
programme.desc: Lucy Worsley explores the character and legacy of the famous monarch through music, considering how Victoria used music to bring the country together during a time of great upheaval and change. Lucy also examines the role music played in the monarch's own life - as a queen, a private person and in her marriage - and how Victoria and Albert established institutions like the Royal College of Music and the Royal Albert Hall. The documentary features performances by Willard White, the BBC Symphony Orchestra and Black Dyke Brass Band

programme.title: Forest, Field & Sky: Art Out of Nature
programme.desc: James Fox journeys through six different landscapes across Britain, meeting artists whose work explores the human relationship with the natural world, from Andy Goldsworthy's stone sculptures to James Turrell's extraordinary Skyspaces

programme.title: Timewatch
programme.desc: Kate Williams explores the story of the monarch who brought Great Britain into the modern age. She examines Victoria's battle to become Queen, in which she fought against her mother, the Duchess of Kent, who wanted to ascend the throne herself - all at a time when the country was on the brink of revolution

programme.title: Queen Victoria: My Musical Britain
programme.desc: Lucy Worsley explores the character and legacy of the famous monarch through music, considering how Victoria used music to bring the country together during a time of great upheaval and change. Lucy also examines the role music played in the monarch's own life - as a queen, a private person and in her marriage - and how Victoria and Albert established institutions like the Royal College of Music and the Royal Albert Hall. The documentary features performances by Willard White, the BBC Symphony Orchestra and Black Dyke Brass Band

programme.title: Fred Dibnah's Made in Britain
programme.desc: Fred travels to the Lake District, which was once one of Britain's biggest sources of iron ore. He also takes a trip across a lake in a steamboat, examines how the rail industry made use of local metals, and finally enjoys a pint with former steelworkers in a local pub

programme.title: The Joy of Painting: Winter Specials
programme.desc: The serenity and silence of a fresh, undisturbed snowfall with Bob Ross, as he takes viewers to a lovely cabin, deep in northern country

programme.title: Live Snooker: The Masters
programme.desc: Stuart Bingham v Shaun Murphy. Further coverage of the fifth day's play at Marshall Arena in Milton Keynes, featuring the second quarter-final. Bingham is the reigning champion, having lifted the trophy for the first time in his career here last year

programme.title: Whisky Galore! (1949)
programme.desc: The residents of a Hebridean island endure many months without alcohol as a result of wartime rationing. When a ship runs aground carrying thousands of cases of Scotch, the locals are determined to keep it to themselves, despite the best efforts of a puritanical English home guard officer to confiscate it. Ealing comedy, starring Basil Radford, Gordon Jackson and James Robertson Justice

programme.title: Mark Kermode's Secrets of Cinema
programme.desc: The critic returns to examines more of the techniques and conventions behind classic movie genres, beginning by looking at the tropes of British comedies. Mark argues that they win laughs by tapping into the public's abiding national preoccupations, from rooting for the underdog to the subversion of class and manners, as well as the audience's fascination with comedy and crime, and pastiches and parodies that take the big beasts of Hollywood down a peg or two

programme.title: Christo and Jeanne-Claude: Monumental Art
programme.desc: Documentary which charts the creation of world-renowned artist Christo's first public work of art in the UK, the Mastaba. In the summer of 2018, on the Serpentine in London's Hyde Park, Christo constructed the Mastaba from 7,506 painted oil barrels. The work was inspired by ancient Mesopotamian tombs and weighs six hundred tonnes. It is the latest work in a career spanning half a century and stretching across the world, and the programme looks back on Christo's career and a life spent making provocative works of art with his wife and partner Jeanne-Claude

programme.title: The Joy of Painting: Winter Specials
programme.desc: The serenity and silence of a fresh, undisturbed snowfall with Bob Ross, as he takes viewers to a lovely cabin, deep in northern country

programme.title: Fred Dibnah's Made in Britain
programme.desc: Fred travels to the Lake District, which was once one of Britain's biggest sources of iron ore. He also takes a trip across a lake in a steamboat, examines how the rail industry made use of local metals, and finally enjoys a pint with former steelworkers in a local pub

programme.title: Queen Victoria's Children
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the reign of the monarch through her relationships with her husband and their children, using letters, diaries, memoirs and journals to bring the subject and characters to life. The first episode focuses on Victoria and Prince Albert's tempestuous marriage, their attempts to engineer the upbringing of their children and their efforts to save the monarchy by projecting a modern image of the royal family

programme.title: Sounds of the Sixties
programme.desc: Manchester never sounded better than Herman's Hermits and the Hollies, who both feature in this archive extravaganza

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1990
programme.desc: Jackie Brambles hosts the edition first aired on June 22, with performances by artists including Magnum, Roxette, Yazz, Big Fun and Sonia, Luciano Pavarotti, Wilson Phillips, MC Hammer and Elton John

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1990
programme.desc: Gary Davies hosts the edition first aired on June 28, with performances by artists including Snap, Jason Donovan, Maureen, Bob Geldof, Bruce Dickinson, Maxi Priest, Elton John and Poison

programme.title: Live Snooker: The Masters

programme.title: Dolly Parton: 50 Years at the Opry
programme.desc: Highlights from the singer's performance to celebrate 50 years as a member of the Grand Ole Opry, recorded in Nashville in October 2019. Featuring hits including Jolene, 9 to 5, Coat of Many Colours and I Will Always Love You, as well as appearances by Lady A, Emmylou Harris and Hank Williams Jr

programme.title: Glastonbury 2014
programme.desc: The country singer's complete set on the Pyramid stage at Worthy Farm in the now-traditional legends spot on Sunday afternoon, featuring Jolene, Coat of Many Colours, 9 to 5, Islands in the Stream and I Will Always Love You

programme.title: Charley Pride - I'm Just Me
programme.desc: The journey of Charley Pride, from his beginnings as a sharecropper's son on a cotton farm in segregated Mississippi to his career as an American League baseball player and his rise as a trailblazing country music superstar. After arriving in Nashville in 1963, thanks to his boldness and perseverance, Pride managed to parlay a series of fortuitous encounters with music industry insiders into a legacy of hit singles. Contributors include Garth Brooks, Dolly Parton, Brad Paisley, Whoopi Goldberg, Willie Nelson, Darius Rucker and Marty Stuart. Narrated by Tanya Tucker

programme.title: Our Musical History
programme.desc: Country duo Ben Earle and Crissie Rhodes reveal the songs that shaped their musical journey. They begin with Dolly Parton's iconic track Jolene. Following that comes Patsy Cline, as Crissie recalls singing along to Crazy with her grandmother. Ben then picks country-pop crossover Shania Twain, whose That Don't Impress Me Much certainly made its mark on him. He also plumps for Glen Campbell's legendary Wichita Lineman. The pair also cite critically acclaimed duo the Civil Wars and their spine-tingling live appearance on Later - with Jools Holland

programme.desc: Lots of repairs are needed at Mister Maker’s home! Kobi from CBeebies’s Apple Tree House is just the friend to help, and Mister Maker video-calls him to reveal his list of jobs! Mister Maker realises that he needs to think up a creative way to thank Kobi and so makes an amazing apple tree picture, using paper loops and pompoms. Later, Mister Maker checks in on his friends, The Shapes. They are snoring away, so Mister M makes sure he leaves them to have a nice rest. Mister Maker then decides to dance like a Shape himself, which in turn inspires him to make some shape art. Mister Maker soon remembers what a gift all of his friends are and this gives him another arty idea - brightly coloured homemade wrapping paper is created! Lastly, Mister Maker remembers all the fun he has had throughout the day.

programme.desc: Bike restorer Tim Gunn brings all his skill to bear on a wartime bicycle once owned and used by a Bletchley Park codebreaker. Horologist Steve Fletcher recruits fellow expert and sister Suzie to work on a neglected and battered Victorian firefighter's helmet. Woodworker Will Kirk and silversmith Brenton West team up to repair a 19th-century stereoscope viewer that sparked a lifelong obsession, and soft toy rest toy restorers Amanda Middleditch and Julie Tatchell try to make a faceless old teddy bear smile again

programme.title: The Truth About
programme.desc: With millions of people swapping the gym for the living room during the Covid-19 pandemic, Mehreen Baig delves into the latest science to find the best way to get fit at home. She uncovers exciting new research testing the theory that just one minute of strength training per week on each main muscle group might reduce your risk of diabetes and heart disease, and also looks at the impact wearable fitness tech actually has on our willpower to stick to an exercise plan. Later, she investigates whether popular supplements like pre-trainers and protein shakes really work

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin helps a couple search for a property in Dorset on a budget of £650,000, and later visits an award-winning dairy farm putting the county's cheeses on the culinary map

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Two couples are preparing for Exeter's Farmers' Market. Ex-archaeologist Janet and her husband Andy are hoping to sell some of their chickens to make enough cash to finish Uncle Pat's new home on their farm. Meanwhile, Liv and Henry are selling their vegetables. They have big plans to create an outdoor kitchen on their modest seven-acre farm, and with some exciting news on the horizon, this young couple are praying for all the sales they can get. Narrated by Denis Lawson

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones presents a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Jean forges ahead with her plan and offers words of wisdom to a struggling Sheree. Shirley is determined to put her family first and wants answers. Martin finally realises what he wants

programme.title: Pooch Perfect
programme.desc: Sheridan Smith and her canine co-host Stanley open the doors of the Pooch Parlour to four more professional dog groomers out to prove their skills. First they tackle a pack of Alaskan malamutes, whose fur well and truly flies when the contestants get to work de-shedding their undercoats. They then give four fluffy puppies their first ever haircut to be revealed in front of their proud owners. Verity Hardcastle and Colin Taylor judge their efforts and decide who is going through to the next round

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: An archaeologist is poisoned during a dig, and with only two suspects and a quick confession, it looks like this is going to be an open-and-shut case. However, it turns out the archaeologists were not alone - and the team realise they have little to no evidence. Meanwhile, Neville and Florence realise they might get along better than they originally thought, although the DI's attempts to embrace island life do not find favour with the Commissioner

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate, inviting a panel of politicians and other guests to answer questions from a virtual audience on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week

programme.title: Supermarket Secrets
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace goes behind the scenes with Britain's biggest food retailers to discover how technology and science are being used to bring consumers their food. In the first episode, Gregg examines the ways that supermarkets are fighting to be first with the latest food fashions, and discovers how online shopping is transforming the way people purchase their food and what they put in their trolleys. He joins Tesco's Head of Plant Innovation as he invents new vegan meals that he claims will taste like meat, and also finds out how Asda are using ingenious growing techniques to make their tomatoes sweeter than strawberries

programme.desc: The travellers still fighting for refunds months after their holidays were cancelled, and a couple asked to pay out for a transatlantic flight to a cruise that never took place

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer

programme.title: The Moment of Proof
programme.desc: How a distinctive deer stalker hat was the red flag to cracking a web of stolen bank cards. Plus, how one careless text message put a dangerous stalker behind bars for the longest sentence in a police force's history. Narrated by Chuku Modu

programme.desc: Andrew Treagust needed a change of pace and thought this lay in New Zealand, where he could follow his dream of becoming a snowboard instructor. Partner Emma was less than convinced, having a job she loved in the UK and two young boys with close ties to their grandparents. Six years after the family sampled a week 'down under', the programme catches up with them to see how their lives have changed. Presented by Nicki Chapman

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: After an early frost decimated a big part of their crop, father-and-son team Billy and Billy Junior are excited to sell a fair chunk of their organic fruit and nuts at Birmingham's Moseley Farmers' Market. These professional athletes pack a whopping punch when it comes to their diet eating up to 4,000 calories when preparing for a bout. It remains to be seen therefore whether Matt's vegan meal can provide them with the energy needed to get them though to an important, upcoming wrestling event

programme.title: The Truth About
programme.desc: With millions of people swapping the gym for the living room during the Covid-19 pandemic, Mehreen Baig delves into the latest science to find the best way to get fit at home. She uncovers exciting new research testing the theory that just one minute of strength training per week on each main muscle group might reduce your risk of diabetes and heart disease, and also looks at the impact wearable fitness tech actually has on our willpower to stick to an exercise plan. Later, she investigates whether popular supplements like pre-trainers and protein shakes really work

programme.title: Scot Squad
programme.desc: The Chief is keen to make amends for a slip of the tongue and keeps digging deeper as his extended apology lasts for ever. The traffic cops make a big noise to salute the key workers, while the rural officers handle a massive marrow bust-up at the horticultural show. Comedy, starring Jack Docherty, Grado and Karen Bartke

programme.title: Father Brown

programme.title: The Moment of Proof
programme.desc: How a satnav revealed the mastermind behind the biggest armed robbery ever to hit Devon and Cornwall, and a hoodie helped police take a dangerous attacker off the streets

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin helps a couple search for a property in Dorset on a budget of £650,000, and later visits an award-winning dairy farm putting the county's cheeses on the culinary map

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Two couples are preparing for Exeter's Farmers' Market. Ex-archaeologist Janet and her husband Andy are hoping to sell some of their chickens to make enough cash to finish Uncle Pat's new home on their farm. Meanwhile, Liv and Henry are selling their vegetables. They have big plans to create an outdoor kitchen on their modest seven-acre farm, and with some exciting news on the horizon, this young couple are praying for all the sales they can get. Narrated by Denis Lawson

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones presents a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Jean forges ahead with her plan and offers words of wisdom to a struggling Sheree. Shirley is determined to put her family first and wants answers. Martin finally realises what he wants

programme.title: Pooch Perfect
programme.desc: Sheridan Smith and her canine co-host Stanley open the doors of the Pooch Parlour to four more professional dog groomers out to prove their skills. First they tackle a pack of Alaskan malamutes, whose fur well and truly flies when the contestants get to work de-shedding their undercoats. They then give four fluffy puppies their first ever haircut to be revealed in front of their proud owners. Verity Hardcastle and Colin Taylor judge their efforts and decide who is going through to the next round

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: An archaeologist is poisoned during a dig, and with only two suspects and a quick confession, it looks like this is going to be an open-and-shut case. However, it turns out the archaeologists were not alone - and the team realise they have little to no evidence. Meanwhile, Neville and Florence realise they might get along better than they originally thought, although the DI's attempts to embrace island life do not find favour with the Commissioner

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate, inviting a panel of politicians and other guests to answer questions from a virtual audience on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week

programme.title: Supermarket Secrets
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace goes behind the scenes with Britain's biggest food retailers to discover how technology and science are being used to bring consumers their food. In the first episode, Gregg examines the ways that supermarkets are fighting to be first with the latest food fashions, and discovers how online shopping is transforming the way people purchase their food and what they put in their trolleys. He joins Tesco's Head of Plant Innovation as he invents new vegan meals that he claims will taste like meat, and also finds out how Asda are using ingenious growing techniques to make their tomatoes sweeter than strawberries

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather. Presented by Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty

programme.title: Rip Off Britain: Holidays
programme.desc: The travellers still fighting for refunds months after their holidays were cancelled, and a couple asked to pay out for a transatlantic flight to a cruise that never took place

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Host Eric Knowles enters the world of travel and transport, as he takes a look back at some of the most interesting past purchases in that genre. He also meets a group of students and their treasured vintage fire engine, dubbed Jezebel, and offers viewers advice on how to spot a bargain

programme.title: Wanted Down Under Revisited
programme.desc: Andrew Treagust needed a change of pace and thought this lay in New Zealand, where he could follow his dream of becoming a snowboard instructor. Partner Emma was less than convinced, having a job she loved in the UK and two young boys with close ties to their grandparents. Six years after the family sampled a week 'down under', the programme catches up with them to see how their lives have changed. Presented by Nicki Chapman

programme.title: Coronavirus: Scotland's Response
programme.desc: Live coverage of the Scottish Government's briefing with reaction, expert analysis and the very latest updates from Scotland and beyond on the pandemic

programme.title: Father Brown

programme.title: The Moment of Proof
programme.desc: How a distinctive deer stalker hat was the red flag to cracking a web of stolen bank cards. Plus, how one careless text message put a dangerous stalker behind bars for the longest sentence in a police force's history. Narrated by Chuku Modu

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Margherita Taylor heads to Rutland, England's smallest county, to help a young mother to carve out a new country life for her family with a budget of £450,000. programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown

programme.title: Pointless

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and a co-host present the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: River City
programme.desc: Lenny makes a final attempt to survive when faced with a vengeful Amber, as time runs out on Alex and Rory's search for their father. Elsewhere, Eve struggles to come to terms that Cameron could be capable of being Poppy's stalker and what she should do next

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Captain Matt Dawson is joined by British athletics stars Adam Gemili and Eilidh Doyle, while Phil Tufnell teams up with former footballers Leon Osman and Stephen Warnock. Sue Barker presents

programme.desc: Denise fears the worst after hearing the truth. Gray struggles with Karen's new role, while a distraught Kathy is at a loss over what to do next

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Snooker player Stephen Hendry, presenter Laura Whitmore and comedian-actors Chris McCausland and Maisie Adam join host Rob Brydon and regular captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show. As usual, the participants set out to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible fibs about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory, through rounds including Home Truths and Quick-fire Lies

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: To celebrate their anniversary, Lee and Lucy plan a friendly night of quizzing with Toby and Anna in their local pub. However, the evening is derailed when neither couple can bear to lose to their partners and the foursome splits into rival teams. Comedy, starring Lee Mack and Sally Bretton, with Hugh Dennis

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: Bodyguard and Durrells star Keeley Hawes joins the host to discuss her new drama series Finding Alice, while writer and one-time Doctor Who co-star Noel Clarke talks about his cop drama Bulletproof: South Africa. Hollywood star Rebel Wilson chats about Straight Talking, which sees her collaborate with former soldier Ant Middleton, and eminent film-maker M Night Shyamalan promotes his new psychological horror series Servant. Comedian Rob Beckett also guests and Jake Bugg performs his current single All I Need

programme.title: Young Adult (2011)
programme.desc: A selfish teenager grows up to be an alcoholic failed writer. When she learns her high-school sweetheart's wife has just had a baby, she refuses to believe he can be happy without her, and returns to her home town to steal him away from his happy family, finding an unlikely ally in a classmate whose adolescence she made a misery. Comedy, starring Charlize Theron, Patrick Wilson and Patton Oswalt

programme.title: Thailand's Youth Rebellion
programme.desc: For five months young protesters in Thailand have been challenging a government backed by the formidable power of the armed forces and the monarchy. For the first time a mass movement is openly calling for reform of the monarchy, shattering a taboo surrounding the royal institution's near-sacred status. Influenced by last year's protests in Hong Kong, young Thais have used social media and drawn on popular culture to outwit the efforts of the Thai police to shut their movement down. An unpopular government has so far been reluctant to use harsh measures against them. The monarchy has lost much of the reverence it enjoyed under the long reign of King Bhumibol, who died four years ago. His less popular son, King Vajiralongkorn, has alarmed many Thais by accumulating even more power and living overseas. But he is now back in Thailand, meeting Thai royalists and encouraging them to challenge the student-led protests with demonstrations of loyalty. The stage is set for a dangerous confronta

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as contestants Aaron, Anne, Susan and Joe pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Meena throws a family drinks do, Marlon makes a special effort, and it remains to be seen whether Sarah will come clean

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Adam tells Carla that Peter plans to refuse a transplant, the twins are underwhelmed with Dev's birthday present, and a police officer questions Tim about the rucksack found in Gary's possession

programme.title: Cornwall and Devon Walks with Julia Bradbury
programme.desc: The presenter walks through Cornwall and Devon, which boast more than 5,000 miles of footpaths, from the rugged rocks of Dartmoor to the picturesque port of Padstow. In the second episode, Julia walks five miles of the stunning north Cornish coast, from popular picturesque Padstow to the surfing hotspot of Trevone Bay. Along the way she experiences dramatic coastal landscapes featured in Poldark, before getting up close to a herd of Fallow Deer in the historic Prideaux Estate

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Jenny tells Peter he needs to find somewhere else to live, as staying in the pub is not helping his alcoholism. Nina urges Asha to not let the likes of Corey bully her, and Tim wonders if Faye knows anything about the attack on Adam

programme.title: The Pembrokeshire Murders
programme.desc: A long-awaited forensic breakthrough gives Detective Steve Wilkins and his team the ammunition they need to finally arrest and prosecute John Cooper. Drama based on the true story of John Cooper, a serial killer who evaded justice for more than 20 years. Starring Luke Evans and Keith Allen

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as contestants Tom, Sonal, Oliver and Heidi pit their wits against quiz genius the Chaser. programme.title: The Pembrokeshire Murders: Catching the Gameshow Killer
programme.desc: Following on from the three-part drama, this documentary tells the real story behind the Pembrokeshire Murders. After murdering four people in Pembrokeshire, John Cooper eluded prosecution for 20 years until a new detective team and advances in forensic science finally saw him put behind bars in 2011. For the first time, the key people involved in the case reveal their role in bringing the serial killer to justice

programme.title: Happy Birthday Mr Bean
programme.desc: How the sitcom character - who featured in just 14 original episodes - has gone on to become a global superstar with an animated series and two top-grossing feature films. This documentary features classic clips and interviews with Richard Curtis, Tony Robinson and Angus Deayton

programme.title: Cornwall and Devon Walks with Julia Bradbury
programme.desc: The presenter walks through Cornwall and Devon, which boast more than 5,000 miles of footpaths, from the rugged rocks of Dartmoor to the picturesque port of Padstow. programme.desc: Cleveland flirts with a co-worker, but Rallo and Junior catch his antics on camera and use them to create a music video that becomes hugely popular on the internet. A major record producer also takes an interest - only to fall in love with Donna

programme.title: The Cleveland Show
programme.desc: One of Rallo's pranks goes too far and leads to Junior getting kicked out of the scouts. A furious Cleveland decides he has finally had enough of his stepson's misbehaviour - and threatens to send him to a young offenders' institution if matters do not improve. Animated sitcom, with the voice of Mike Henry

programme.title: Iain Stirling's CelebAbility
programme.desc: Famous faces Laura Whitmore, Louis Smith, Stephanie Pratt, Sair Khan and Ed Gamble take on five friends in a series of challenges. Game show, hosted by Iain Stirling

programme.title: Hey Tracey!
programme.desc: Joel Dommett hosts as Russell Kane, Jorgie Porter, Paige Turley, Suzi Ruffell, Radzi Chinyanganya and David Potts try to win some normal people £2,500 and a pet-food dispenser

programme.title: Totally Bonkers Guinness World Records
programme.desc: Matt Edmondson introduces the records for putting on hair rollers while on a rollercoaster and regurgitating coins, and sees the world's biggest collection of Star Wars memorabilia

programme.title: ITV2 Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Kevin tells Natalie there is no hope of saving his marriage. Samantha is driven to distraction by the Battersbys. Gail is amazed when Roy gives her tickets to take Martin to Paris

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Fiona is disgusted with herself for sleeping with Jim. Steve is amazed when Vicky's solicitor offers him a huge divorce settlement. Zoe has a shocking proposal for Judy and Gary

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: The Hutchinsons arrive at the Woolpack with their few remaining possessions. Chris tells the Dingles that he has found the body and is going to call the police

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Tara is confused by Biff's cold behaviour towards her. Steve is ambushed by his fellow workers at the quarry. Terry takes Will to a rugby trial at Farsley Rugby Club

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Joe and Carol join forces to unravel the mystery of a girl resident at Throckton Grange, when she collapses with exhaustion and expresses a fear of being taken away. Meanwhile, Geoff suffers from embarrassing bouts of sneezing when he tries to speak to Lucy who is temporarily working at the village shop, and David is surprised to be offered an unusual post at the local newspaper. Simon Shepherd and Victor McGuire guest star

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Mike and Sally plan a holiday, but things run far from smoothly when Sid gets involved with the financial planning. Comedy, starring Sid James and Diana Coupland

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: The Ropers take in a lodger, and Mildred is delighted when he begins doing all the household jobs that George will not tackle. Comedy, starring Brian Murphy and Yootha Joyce

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: As the Second World War continues to rage, Foyle investigates a pro-Hitler organisation he suspects may be linked to a mysterious death at the White Feather Hotel, while reluctant assistant Milner struggles to decide where his loyalties lie. Crime drama, starring Michael Kitchen and Anthony Howell, with Charles Dance and Maggie Steed

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: The plight of two young girls is brought to the attention of Carol, who discovers them home alone. Joe confronts their parents who are out drinking, but finds stepfather Brian unremorseful and reluctant to leave the pub. Gina is distracted from her worries about her unborn baby by preparations for the imminent arrival of Phil's niece Dawn, but when David goes to meet the girl from the station she is nowhere to be seen. Nikki Sanderson joins the cast

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Steve is determined to go to work despite being beaten up. Roy gives Kelly some home truths. Kathy is sure Eric has plans to ruin her

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kathy struggles to cope with breakfasts at the tearooms. Kim receives the vet's bill for Kaiser. Heather pours her heart out to Terry

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Gary tells Judy they cannot buy Zoe's baby as it is illegal. Les plans to make a pass at Samantha. Jim tells Fiona he cannot get her out of his mind

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Alan is stunned when Fiona suggests they get married as soon as possible. Zoe lets Audrey jump to the conclusion that Gary is the father of her baby

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A fox-hunt turns sour when a tramp is found dead in the woods, and matters are further complicated by the murder of the chief suspect's father. As DCI Barnaby investigates, he concludes it is only a matter of time before another member of the tight-knit community suffers the same fate. Guest starring James Bolam

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Aircraft manufacturer Lord Mayfield hosts a party to trap a woman suspected of being a spy for the Germans. Fearing events might not go according to plan, Lady Mayfield decides to summon the famous Belgian detective to keep an eye on proceedings. Starring David Suchet, with John Stride and Phyllida Law

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: The detective applies his professional know-how to a fatal shooting, and soon begins to suspect that the dead man's brother-in-law may know more about the case than he is letting on - a theory borne out by forensic tests on the murder weapon. Jack Shepherd stars as the dour detective, with Helen Masters and Jimmy Yuill

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A young woman asks Poirot to investigate her father's murder - a 14-year-old crime for which her mother was found guilty and executed. The detective agrees to investigate, but warns his client that he may discover things she does not want to hear. David Suchet stars, with Rachael Stirling, Aidan Gillen, Aimee Mullens, Toby Stephens, Gemma Jones, Patrick Malahide and Annette Badland

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: The unassuming detective launches an investigation when a conscientious objector dies while in police custody, and the judge who ordered his arrest is nearly killed in his booby-trapped home. His inquiries uncover a web of lies and corruption in the heart of wartime Hastings. Crime drama, starring Michael Kitchen and David Tennant

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: A gathering at the Devon estate of Miss Marple's old school friend Lady Tressilian leads to murder when the eccentric Mr Treves tells the story of an unconvicted child killer. Miss Marple is on hand to assist Supt Mallard of the local constabulary, but not before a second victim is claimed. Mystery, starring Geraldine McEwan, with guest appearances by Eileen Atkins, Tom Baker, Paul Nicholls, Alan Davies and Greg Wise, with a cameo by former British number-one tennis player Greg Rusedski
